paper_title: Climate and Fire-Season Analysis in Quebec-Labrador

methods.method_name: Climate Data Processing and Fire-Season Length Calculation
methods.description: This method involves processing climate simulation data to compute fire-season lengths over the last 7000 years using the HadCM3 climate model. It includes downscaling climate data, reconstructing time series, and calculating the Drought Code index to assess fire risks.

steps.description: Compute the twelve monthly temperature and precipitation anomalies for each millennium of the HadCM3 dataset compared to the pre-industrial control (1750 AD).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate monthly temperature and precipitation anomalies from HadCM3 dataset

steps.description: Downscale the Holocene datasets at 0.5° resolution by applying HadCM3 temperature and precipitation anomalies to modern 1971-2000 climate normals.
steps.searchable_summary: downscale Holocene datasets using HadCM3 anomalies and modern climate normals

steps.description: Reconstruct 30-year time series for each 0.5° pixel using Gaussian distribution for temperature and Gamma distribution for precipitation.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct 30-year climate time series using statistical distributions

steps.description: Apply the weather generator to derive daily values from the reconstructed 30-year monthly temperature and precipitation time-series.
steps.searchable_summary: generate daily climate data from monthly time series

steps.description: Compute the Drought Code index (DC index) using the daily values to assess fire risks.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate Drought Code index from daily climate data

steps.description: Calculate the fire-season length based on the cumulative number of days with mean DC index value >80 units.
steps.searchable_summary: determine fire-season length using DC index threshold

steps.description: Determine spring fire onset, summer fire termination, and fire season length in spring, summer, and over the year.
steps.searchable_summary: identify fire season onset and termination periods

steps.description: Compute correlations between reconstructed climate outputs and fire indices using Pearson's correlation coefficients and permutation tests.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlations between climate data and fire indices
